# 🚚 Sprint <n> — Deliverable <n> — <name>

## 1. 🧭 TL;DR

State what this deliverable changes in repo or runtime truth, what already exists on the branch, and what completion means in operational terms.

## 2. 📌 Deliverable outcome

- exact outcome
- why this deliverable exists now
- what later work may assume after it lands

## 3. 🔍 Current repo baseline

### 3.1 ✅ Already true

- structures, controls, or runtimes already present

### 3.2 ⚠️ Remaining drift or gap

- gaps that still make the deliverable incomplete
- stale assumptions that must be removed

## 4. 🎯 Success definition

- reviewer-visible truths that must be answerable from the repo itself
- measurable or executable completion checks
- failure conditions that still count as incomplete

## 5. 📏 Frozen decisions

Link to owner docs and ADRs instead of repeating full policy text.

- decisions this deliverable inherits unchanged
- boundaries that later tasks must not reopen

## 6. 🗂️ Target surface

- expected files, folders, or docs after completion
- exact bounded scope for any new reusable artifacts

## 7. 🎟️ Task breakdown

For each task include:

- exact goal
- touched surfaces
- dependency order
- handoff output expected for the next task or lane

## 8. ✅ Verification gates

- baseline repo-control command
- full gate command when required
- focused test commands for touched surfaces
- evidence expectations for docs, workflows, or contracts

## 9. 🚫 Non-goals

- tempting adjacent work that stays out of scope
- runtime or policy changes deferred to later deliverables

## 10. 📂 Governed files

List protected paths, owner docs, templates, verifier files, and runtime boundaries directly governed by this deliverable.

## 11. 🧩 Execution handoff

- next bounded implementation target
- inherited assumptions
- unresolved but in-scope follow-up items
